turns.user_message: I noticed lately that I catch myself replaying every little interaction in my head, dissecting the exact wording people used with me. It really just started a couple days ago, but it feels exhausting already. I keep wondering if I said something wrong or came across too intense. Honestly, it is making me second guess even the simplest texts I send out. I just wish I could stop overanalyzing everything and actually relax when I am talking to people.

It has been happening almost every single day now, and the constant mental replay is completely draining my energy. I find myself drafting and redrafting simple replies just to make sure I do not accidentally push anyone away. Every time I see a delayed response, my stomach drops and I assume I messed up somehow. I am trying so hard to keep things light, but the weight of it is just sitting heavy on my chest. I do not know how to turn off this constant need to monitor how I am being perceived.

The whole thing has completely taken over my headspace, and I feel like I am constantly bracing for disappointment before anyone even speaks. I am so terrified of saying the wrong thing that I have started pulling back from conversations altogether, which just leaves me feeling incredibly isolated. It is like I am trapped in this loop where I am desperate for connection but convinced I am going to ruin it the moment I try. I keep staring at my screen, paralyzed by the fear that my presence is just too much for anyone to handle. I really just want to feel normal around people again without this heavy knot in my stomach.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This just started recently.
